assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md contains '## Classification: code change request' and 'Action: Sub-task created to address this feedback.' The report table confirms: '30001 | code change request | Sub-task created'. subtask-1.md exists and references comment 30001.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: suggestion' and explains: 'The reviewer proposes adding a partial index on deleted_at for performance optimization. The language uses "should also add" which could be interpreted as directive, but the core recommendation is a performance optimization rather than a correctness fix.' The reasoning explicitly addresses the suggestive language.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md contains '## Classification: nit' and 'Action: No sub-task created.' The report confirms: '30003 | nit | No sub-task'. No subtask file references comment 30003.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md contains '## Classification: question' and 'Action: No sub-task created.' The report confirms: '30004 | question | No sub-task'. No subtask file references comment 30004.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 19). All sections have substantive content.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 32) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 33).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 27) with the original comment text quoted: 'Reviewer @reviewer-a (comment 30001) on modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs line 60: > The soft_delete method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-1.md), and a report (report.md). None of these are code files. No evidence of code file modifications in any output.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Comment Classifications table lists all four comments with their classifications. The report header states '4 review comments classified'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's overall verdict is 'FAIL' and contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The report only identifies issues and creates sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are new additions (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file with 5 test functions) |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md explicitly addresses convention upgrade: 'This was evaluated for convention upgrade (Step 6b) by the Style/Conventions sub-agent. The repository's CONVENTIONS.md content was not available for inspection, so no documented convention match could be established. Without concrete evidence from a CONVENTIONS.md section or a counted codebase pattern... the suggestion cannot be upgraded to a code change request per the verify-pr policy.' It lists three specific reasons why upgrade was not applied.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'Action: No sub-task created.' The report confirms: '30002 | suggestion | No sub-task (no convention evidence for upgrade)'. Only subtask-1.md exists in outputs, and it references comment 30001, not 30002.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | All 5 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected; test file is new and additive. Eval Quality: N/A |'. The Test Quality row explicitly states 'Eval Quality: N/A'.
